segments.s633-g9: به نزدیک + noun forms a locative prepositional phrase meaning 'near/close to'. آن کرگ is the noun phrase 'that bird/kerag' (آن = 'that'). باید شدن is a compound expressing necessity or destined occurrence: باید (must/ought) + شدن (to happen/become) yields 'must happen / was bound to happen'; the verb appears at the end as is normal in Persian narrative word order.
segments.s633-g10: همه چرم او را is the definite object phrase 'all his hide/skin' with را marking it as object. به تیر آژدن is an instrumental/agent phrase 'by the dragon's arrow' (به used here with the sense 'by'). Word order places the object before the agent phrase and the agent after به.
segments.s633-g11: اگر introduces a conditional 'if'. ز (poetic/archaic preposition) + pronoun (here written as زو in the running text) means 'from/because of him' or 'on his account'. تهی گردد is the predicate: تهی 'empty' + گردد (subjunctive/optative of گشت/گردیدن 'become'), so 'should become empty'. این بوم و بر ('this bōm and bar') is the affected territory — a set phrase meaning 'this land and realm'.
segments.s633-g12: به فر تو places the beneficiary/instrument before the person: به here means 'by/under/for' (instrumental/benefactive) and فر is the Zoroastrian/epic concept of royal radiance or fortune ('farr'). تو is the possessor 'your'. این مرد پیروزگر 'this victorious man' is the person being assigned; the verb is elliptical/understood (e.g. 'let/make him be for your farr'). Word order emphasizes 'به فر تو' before the noun phrase.
segments.s633-g13: یکی here is an indefinite 'one/some(one)', followed by دست meaning a 'hand' in the sense of help or helper. باشدت is an older optative/subjunctive form (باشد + enclitic -ت) expressing a wish — 'may there be for you' — so the phrase reads roughly 'may one hand be for you'. The locative نزدیک من follows and links to the preceding noun phrase, giving the place/proximity of that helper: 'near me.'
segments.s633-g14: چه is an exclamatory 'how' here, intensifying the following adjective. نزدیک is the adjective 'near', and این نامدار انجمن is the noun phrase 'this renowned assembly/court' (نامدار = renowned; انجمن = assembly/gathering). The order is poetic (adjective + noun), so the segment reads as an exclamation: 'How near is this renowned assembly!'
segments.s633-g15: که is the subordinating/relative 'that' linking this clause to what precedes. جاوید is an adjective 'everlasting/eternal' placed before the locative phrase; در + کشور هندوان is the prepositional phrase 'in the country/realm of the Hindus.' Together this segment sets the domain/timeframe: 'that (it) was/should be everlasting in the land of the Hindus.'
segments.s633-g16: بود is the copula in the past/finite sense here ('was' / used to state existence); زنده ('living, alive') modifies نام ('name') and the possessive pronoun تو follows the noun (classical order: 'زنده نام تو' = 'your name alive'). تا جاودان means 'until eternity / forever' and expresses the intended long duration. The whole segment therefore states: 'your name was (or would be) living until eternity.'
segments.s633-g25: همی گفت is an archaic/progressive verbal phrase: همی is a continuity/emphasis particle ('kept/was') placed before the verb گفت ('said'), so together it means 'kept saying' or 'was saying.' چندی is an adverb meaning 'for a while' and z (ز) is the poetic short form of از introducing the topic/source ('of' / 'about') followed by آرام اوی = 'his calmness' (آرام noun + اوی, archaic possessive 'his'). The whole group functions as a single clause: 'He kept saying for a while about his calmness.'
segments.s633-g26: This is a coordinated continuation introduced again with the prepositional/partitive ز 'of/about' followed by a list of nouns joined by و 'and' (بالا 'height/loftiness', پهنا 'breadth/expanse', اندام 'figure/body') and closed by the possessive اوی 'his.' Grammatically this continues the preceding remark: 'about his height and breadth and figure.'
segments.s633-g27: چو here is the subordinating conjunction 'when.' بنمود is a past tense of نمودن (to show/appear) used here as 'appeared' or 'showed himself.' It is coordinated with برگشت 'returned/turned back' by و 'and', and then the clause concludes with بهرام رفت ('Bahram went'), where رفت is the simple past 'went.' Word order is typical older Persian: temporal subordinate (چو + verb), then coordinated actions, then the main subject and verb.
segments.s633-g28: خرامان is an adverb/adjectival participle meaning 'with a proud/stately gait' and modifies the following verb رفت (understood here as 'went striding'). The cluster ب + دان here forms بِدان = 'to that' (poetic contraction of به + آن). بیشه 'thicket/grove' is the noun followed by کرگ 'of down/nap' (a descriptive genitive) and تفت (past/heated) used adjectivally 'scorched/charred.' The whole segment describes Bahram's manner and destination: 'striding into that scorched, downy thicket.'
segments.s633-g29: پس is a temporal/consecutive adverb 'then/afterwards.' پشت او is a prepositional phrase 'behind him' (پشت + pronoun او). چند ایرانیان places چند 'several/a few' before the plural noun ایرانیان 'Iranians' — typical Persian adjective/numeral + noun order. The whole phrase means 'then, behind him, several Iranians...' and sets the actors/position for the following action.
segments.s633-g30: به پیکار is a set phrase 'into/for battle' (به + پیکار). آن کرگ 'that krag' is the direct object (آن = that + noun). بسته is an adjectival/past participle 'bound/tied' (used predicatively), and میان 'between/in the midst' completes the spatial relation. Together the clause reports the Iranians' action in battle: 'they bound that krag between them / in the midst.'
segments.s633-g31: چو 'when' introduces a temporal clause. از دور دیدند is a verbal phrase 'they saw from a distance' (از دور = from afar; دیدند = they saw). خرطوم اوی is the noun + possessive 'his trunk' (خرطوم = trunk/proboscis; اوی = his). The segment is a complete temporal subordinate: 'When from afar they saw his trunk...'.
segments.s633-g32: ز here functions as 'because of' (introducing cause). هنگش is هنگ + possessive suffix 'his tumult/commotion' (هنگ = commotion/tumult; -ش = his). همی before the verb شد adds immediacy/emphasis (here rendered as 'was/indeed became'); پست شد is the compound passive/simple past 'became low' (پست = low; شد = became). بوم اوی is a genitive 'his bōm' (bōm can mean homeland/soil/dwelling), with اوی again the possessive. The clause literally reads 'because of his commotion his bōm became low.'
segments.s633-g65: برفتند is the past, 3rd-person plural of رفتن with the prefix بر- (literally 'went/advanced up'); it opens the clause and indicates that they approached. هر before مهتری is the distributive 'each/every', and مهتری here means 'a chief/elder'. با نثار is a prepositional phrase 'with offerings/tribute' (با = with, نثار = offering) describing what they bore as they advanced.
segments.s633-g66: به introduces the indirect object 'to' (to Bahram). گفتند is the past 3rd-person plural of گفتن 'they said' (here used to introduce direct speech). کای is the archaic/votive particle 'O' (used to address someone), and نامدار is an adjective 'renowned/illustrious' that follows the vocative.
segments.s633-g67: کسی را ... نیست is an existential negation meaning 'there is no one'. را is the definite/object/topic marker attached to کسی. سزای is a noun 'desert/recompense' (what is deserved), modified by تو کردار 'your deed(s)' (تو = your, کردار = deed). نیست is the negative copula 'does not exist/is not'.
segments.s633-g68: به کردار تو is a prepositional phrase meaning 'by/according to your deeds' (به used here as 'by' or 'by means of'). راه دیدار نیست is a nominal statement 'there is no road/way of meeting' (راه = way/path, دیدار = meeting/seeing), with نیست concluding the clause as the negative copula.
segments.s633-g69: ازو is the older contracted form of از او 'from/of him'. شادمان is 'joyful' or 'rejoicing'. شنگل is an obscure/poetic word here paired with شادمان (left untranslated in the lexical list; treated as a descriptive term). و links the clauses, and دل به غم literally 'heart to sorrow' means 'a heart given over to grief' (به = 'to/for', غم = 'sorrow').
segments.s633-g70: گهی is an adverb 'sometimes'. تازه‌روی describes a fresh/new appearance ('fresh-faced' / 'newcomer' sense in this context). و زمانی links the contrastive time phrase ('and at times' / 'for a while'), with دژم an adjective 'sullen/grim' describing his demeanor in those moments.
segments.s633-g41: This clause literally orders as prepositional phrase + noun phrase + interrogative verbless construction: 'به جای دگر' (in another place) precedes 'مرگ من' (my death). 'چون بود' is an older/poetic copular question ‘how was (it)’ — here asking what my death would have been like. Word order is inverted for poetic effect (normal prose: «مرگ من در جای دگر چگونه بود»).
segments.s633-g42: 'که' introduces a causal/explanatory clause ('for/since'). 'اندیشه' is the subject 'thought/intention.' The preposition 'ز' (poetic form of از) with 'اندازه بیرون' literally 'outside of measure' forms the predicate 'بیرون بود' = 'was beyond measure' (بود = past copula). The whole clause explains the reason: his thought exceeded proper bounds.
segments.s633-g43: The direct object 'کمان را' is marked with the object marker 'را'. 'به زه کرد' is a light-verb construction meaning 'did (to the) string' — i.e., 'strung' or 'drew the bow'; 'مرد جوان' (the young man) is the agent and appears after the verb, a common poetic inversion of SOV order. 'کرد' is the simple past light verb 'did/made'.
segments.s633-g44: 'تو گفتی' is the 2nd-person past 'you said' introducing reported speech; (the dictionary senses given do not list the verbal past form 'you said', so see senses mapping). 'همی' is a habitual/emphatic particle ('even/indeed/still') giving immediacy. 'خوار' is a noun/adjective 'the despised' (understood noun phrase), 'گیرد' is a present/future-form verb meaning 'seize/take' (here used to report what was said would happen), and 'روان' is the object 'soul/spirit'. The clause thus reports: 'you said (that) the despised would seize the soul.'
segments.s633-g45: 'بیامد' is the past 'he came.' 'دوان' is a verbal/adverbial form 'running/rushed' modifying the verb ('he came running'). 'تا' functions as a limit-marker 'until/to' and 'به نزدیک کرگ' gives the endpoint 'to near Kerag' (نزدیک with به to indicate vicinity). The subject is implicit (carried over from prior narrative).
segments.s633-g46: 'پر از خشم' is an adjectival phrase 'full of anger' (پر = full, followed by از + noun). 'سر دل' is a poetic compound roughly 'the inward head/heart' (سر here = inner thought/secret). 'نهاده' is a past participle 'having placed / set' used predicatively, and 'به مرگ' ('toward death' or 'for death') indicates the aim — i.e., he resolved or set his heart upon death. The clause describes his mood and determined intent.
segments.s633-g47: 'کمان کیانی' is a genitival/attributive phrase 'the Kayanian (royal) bow' (modifier after noun, poetic usage). 'گرفته' is a perfect/past-participial form 'having taken/seized' describing action; 'به چنگ' is an idiom meaning 'into the hand/grip' (چنگ literally 'claw' but used figuratively as 'grasp'). Together: 'having taken the Kayanian bow in his grip.'
segments.s633-g48: The preposition 'ز' (poetic form of 'از') means 'from.' Here it governs 'ترکش' (quiver) — note the orthographic split in the wordlist (ترک + ش): the suffix 'ش' is a 3rd-person possessive clitic 'his/its.' 'برآورد' is a past verb meaning 'took out/drew forth' (here used as a simple past action), and 'تیر خدنگ' is the direct object 'a dagger-like arrow' (تیر = arrow, خدنگ = dagger-like/short blade).
segments.s633-g33: بدو is the prepositional clitic meaning “to him/unto him” and appears before the subject for emphasis. هرکسی here functions as a generic/everyone subject (“everyone”); گفت is the past tense verb “said” (classical past) but its listed noun senses don't match the verbal use (so treat it as the ordinary past of گفتن). شاها is the vocative “O King!” and مکن is the negative imperative ‘do not’ addressed to the king.
segments.s633-g34: ز here is the poetic preposition “from/than” (introducing the standard from which something departs). مردی is used abstractly as “manliness / what is manly” rather than a concrete man. همی is the archaic/historical continuous/present marker that often accompanies classical verbs; together همی بگذرد gives an intransitive present/factual sense “passes/exceeds.” این سخن is the topical demonstrative “this speech/remark.” The phrase literally: “this remark passes beyond (what is) manliness.”
segments.s633-g35: نکردست is a literary/periphrastic past meaning “has not done/there has not been”; it negates existence of the action. کس here is used in the negative sense “no one.” جنگ با کوه و سنگ is a poetic hyperbole “war against mountain and stone,” with با introducing the objects “with/against.” The clause asserts that nobody has engaged in such a clash.
segments.s633-g36: وگرچه is the concessive conjunction “although / even though.” دلیرست is an old-style copular adjective meaning “is brave.” خسرو is the proper name (the subject). به + چنگ here is an adverbial phrase; classical Persian uses به چنگ metaphorically to indicate ‘in hand, in grasp, in close combat,’ so the sense is ‘brave in hand/when grasping or fighting.’
segments.s633-g37: به شنگل چنین گوی is an imperative construction: به + name marks the addressee (‘to Shangal’), چنین ‘thus/so’ qualifies the manner, and گوی is the (archaic) imperative of گفتن ‘say/tell’ (here used as a command). کاین راه نیست is a subordinate reported clause introduced by کاین (= که این) meaning “that this ...,” with راه نیست stating “this path/way does not exist / this is not the way.”
segments.s633-g38: بدین here functions as a demonstrative/adverbial “by this / in this (matter)” and in the manuscript is segmented oddly, but the intended sense is “in this [war].” جنگ دستوری: جنگ ‘battle’ + دستوری ‘commanding/ by command’ — together meaning a battle that is commanded/authorized; شاه نیست asserts that there is no kingly command or the king is not in command. The clause thus denies royal authorization for the engagement.
segments.s633-g39: چنین داد پاسخ is a fixed phrase “gave this answer / replied thus,” where داد is the past tense verb ‘gave’ used with پاسخ ‘answer.’ که introduces the content of the reply (‘that’ / ‘that which’). یزدان پاک = ‘the pure God’ (subject of the conditional clause to follow).
segments.s633-g40: مرا‌گر is a fused classical conditional phrase meaning literally ‘if to me’ or ‘if ... were mine’ (here introducing a counterfactual: ‘if He had given me’). به هندوستان داد خاک literally ‘gave the soil to India / gave me the soil of India’ — به هندوستان = ‘of/for India’ (here: ‘the soil of India’), داد is the past ‘gave,’ and خاک is ‘earth/soil.’ Together they form the protasis: ‘if the pure God had given me the soil of India…’
segments.s633-g49: همی is a classical/perf. iterative marker here giving a continuous or repeated action ('kept'). تیر is the grammatical subject placed before the verb (poetic word order). بارید is simple past 'rained/fell' used metaphorically for arrows. همچون تگرگ is a post-verbal simile 'like hail' modifying the manner of falling.
segments.s633-g50: برین همنشان = بر + این + همنشان (poetic orthography) = 'upon these fellow-sitters/peers'; the preposition بر (on/upon) combines with the demonstrative ین (= این). تا introduces a limit 'until'; غمین گشت is a two-word predicate: غمین (adjective 'sorrowful') followed by گشت (past of گشتن 'became'). کرگ is the definite object of سر later (a bird/creature); here it is the thing that 'became sorrowful.'
segments.s633-g51: چو here means 'when' introducing a temporal clause. دانست is the past tense 'he knew/realized.' کو را is an archaic/poetic sequence (here functioning to mark the person/instance whose time has come) followed by the object marker را; سرآمد زمان is a nominal phrase 'the end/coming of the time' (i.e. the appointed moment).
segments.s633-g52: برآهیخت is a compound past verb 'he rose up / he lifted (himself)' (poetic past). خنجر is the direct object 'dagger.' به جای کمان is a prepositional phrase 'in the place of the bow / instead of the bow' with به + جای forming the fixed expression 'in place of.' Word order (verb then object) reflects epic/poetic flexibility.
segments.s633-g53: سر کرگ را marks a definite object ('the head of the kerk') with را after the possessive phrase. راست functions adverbially 'straight/cleanly' modifying the verb. ببرید is the (poetic) past form 'cut/severed' (the expected root is بریدن). و گفت connects actions: 'and he said.' Note: some lexical senses provided in the sheet do not include the verbal reading of ببرید/گفت, so those are irregular/poetic usages.
segments.s633-g54: به نام is a standard prepositional collocation 'in the name of' (به functioning as the preposition). خداوند is the head noun 'God' and بی‌یار و جفت are coordinated epithets following the noun ('without peer/companion and without match'), a fixed honorific formula. The whole phrase functions as the direct quotation's opening formula.
segments.s633-g55: که introduces a subordinate clause 'for/that.' او is the subject 'he.' داد here is the verbal sense 'gave.' چندین is a multiplicative quantifier 'many a / several.' مرا (written here as م + را) is the first-person object 'me' (م + object marker را). فر و زور are coordinated direct objects 'farr (royal glory) and strength/might' — the things given.
segments.s633-g56: به فرمان او is a prepositional phrase 'by his command' (به + فرمان + possessive او). تابد is a present-tense or gnomic verb 'shines' (تافتن root) agreeing with the granted qualities (فر و زور) as their result. از چرخ هور is a genitive/ablative phrase 'from the sky/heaven of Hvar' (از indicating source, چرخ = sky/fate, هور = splendour/Hvar).
segments.s633-g57: بفرمود is an honorific/past reporting verb meaning 'he commanded/said' (3rd person past). تا introduces a subordinate purpose/command clause ('that/so that'); برند is the present-subjunctive/optative 3rd-person plural (ب‑ + رَوند/بردند-type stem) used after تا to express what they should do. گاو و گردون are coordinated direct objects of برند (the second noun is a poetic/epic noun placed in coordination).
segments.s633-g58: This clause completes the command: سر کرگ is the noun phrase ('the head of کرگ') serving as the object of برند. زان + بیشه is a prepositional phrase ('from that grove'); بیرون is an adverb/adverbial ('out, away'); برند (repeated) is again the subjunctive/optative verb 'carry/bring' placed at the end — typical Persian word order puts the verb after its objects and modifiers.
segments.s633-g59: ببردند is a past 3rd-person-plural verb 'they carried/brought' (the verbal prefix ب- appears in many classical/performance forms). چون here functions as a temporal conjunction 'when/as' introducing the subordinate clause. دید is the past tense 'saw' with شنگل as subject; ز دور is a prepositional phrase 'from afar'.
segments.s633-g60: به introduces the manner/material with which the iwan is adorned ('with/in brocade'); دیبا is the instrument/material 'brocade'; بیاراست is a past tense of آراستن with the prefix بی- ('he adorned/dressed'); ایوان سور is a genitive or attributive phrase 'the iwan of the feast' (سور = feast), the whole phrase is the object/place being adorned.
segments.s633-g61: چو is the temporal conjunction 'when'; بر تخت بنشست is a verb phrase with the preposition بر 'on/upon' + تخت 'throne' and بنشست (past) 'sat (down)' — 'he sat upon the throne'. پرمایه is an epithet modifying شاه ('the rich/substantial/valiant king').
segments.s633-g62: نشاندند is past 3rd-person-plural 'they seated/placed (someone)'; بهرام را is the definite object marked by را (he is what was seated); پیش گاه is a prepositional/nominal phrase meaning 'before the court' or 'in the royal presence' (پیش = before/in the presence of, گاه = place/occasion). The verb comes before the object-marker phrase, as is normal in Persian.
segments.s633-g63: همی کرد is a habitual/continuous past construction (همی + past verb) meaning 'would/do repeatedly' or 'kept doing'; هر کس is 'each person/anyone'; برو آفرین is an idiomatic exclamation here translated 'Bravo!' — برو functions as part of the fixed acclamation rather than a literal imperative 'go'.
segments.s633-g64: This is a nominative/agent phrase naming those who cried: بزرگان هند ('the great men/nobles of India') و سواران ('and the horsemen'). چنین here is a sentence-level adverb 'thus/so', summarizing that those groups performed the same acclamation.
segments.s633-g17: This is an inverted clause: بدو (to him) is an indirect‑object prepositional element placed before the verb گفت. گفت is the past tense verb 'said' and the following proper name بهرام (Bahram) is the grammatical subject; پاکیزه‌رای is a postpositive epithet ('pure‑minded') modifying Bahram.
segments.s633-g18: که introduces a content/complaint clause 'that...'. با من literally 'with me' (i.e. accompanying me). بباید is a modal verb 'must/ought to' (here expressing the speaker's need). یکی رهنمای is an indefinite noun phrase 'one/a certain guide' functioning as the complement of بباید.
segments.s633-g19: چو here is the temporal conjunction 'when'. بینم is first‑person present/subjunctive used in a temporal clause meaning 'I see/behold'. به functions as a preposition 'by' with نیروی یزدان 'the power of God' (agentive/instrumental), and تنش is 'his body' (تن + enclitic pronoun ش).
segments.s633-g20: ببینی is second‑person present/subjunctive used here with a future sense 'you will see'. به before خون gives the sense 'in/with' (i.e. soaked in blood). غرقه is a participial/adjectival form 'drenched/immersed', modifying پیراهنش 'his tunic/shirt' (پیراهن + possessive ش).
segments.s633-g21: بدو داد is 'gave to him' (بدو = 'to him', داد = past 'gave'); شنگل is an item/name offered (an obscure term here, treated as the thing/person given). یکی رهنمای is an appositive 'a certain guide' describing شنگل.
segments.s633-g22: که introduces a relative clause 'whom/which'. او را is the topical/object marker construction ('him' + را). نشیمن is 'seat/dwelling/place' and بدانست is a past verb 'knew it/considered it' — here 'he regarded him/it as (his) seat', followed by و جای 'and place' (repeating the idea).
segments.s633-g23: همی رفت is a habitual/progressive past 'he went/continued on' (همی marking continuity). با introduces the companion 'with', and نیک‌دل رهنمون is a noun phrase 'the kind‑hearted guide' (نیک‌دل qualifying رهنمون).
segments.s633-g24: بدان here functions as an adverbial 'to that' (classical be + an/آن = 'to that' or 'into that'). بیشه is 'thicket/grove'. کرگ ریزنده خون is a noun + participle phrase: ریزنده is a present participle 'shedding/dripping' modifying خون 'blood'; کرگ is the noun being described (an archaic/local word — contextually a predatory creature, often read as 'wolf' in classic texts).
segments.s633-g1: یکی here functions as an indefinite determiner 'a certain'. The noun کرگ (a vulture/bird) is the subject, followed by the past copula بود ('was')—classical word order where the verb follows the subject. اندرَان شهرِ شاه is a locative phrase placed after the verb: اندرَان (= 'in/therein') + شهرِ شاه ('the king's city') gives the location.
segments.s633-g2: ز بالای او = 'from/on top of him' (ز = poetic 'from', بالای = 'top of/on'), او = 'it/him'. بسته is a past participle/adjectival form 'tied/bound'. بر باد راه = 'a trail/streamer on the wind' (بر = 'on/upon', باد = 'wind', راه = 'path/trail'); together it describes something fastened to the bird's back that trailed in the wind. The phrase uses archaic/prepositional order and participial adjective after the noun phrase.
segments.s633-g3: ازان بیشه = 'from that grove/thicket' (ازان = archaic 'from that/thereof' before the noun). بگریختی is a past escape/flight verb; in classical verse the verbal form with final -ی can appear where modern prose would use a different ending, and the verb may precede or follow its subject. شیر نر is the subject 'the male lion' (noun + post‑posed modifier).
segments.s633-g4: هم = 'also/too' introduces the second, parallel arrival. از آسمان = 'from the sky'. کرگس تیرپر is a compound nominal phrase: کرگس = 'vulture' and تیرپر used adjectivally ('arrow‑feathered' / 'feather‑winged'), describing the bird; the verb is omitted here (elliptical) and understood from the previous clause ('came'/'appeared').
segments.s633-g5: یکایک = 'one by one' / 'each and every', placed before the group noun to distribute it. همه هند = 'all of Hind' (the people/land). زو is an archaic contraction of 'از او' (= 'because of him' / 'from him')—here formed from ز + و; پر خروش = 'full of tumult' (پر = 'full (of)', خروش = 'uproar/cry'), so the whole segment says that all in Hind, one and all, were full of uproar because of him.
segments.s633-g6: از آواز او = 'from/because of his voice' (از introducing cause, آواز + pronoun او). کر شدی is 'became deaf' where کر ('deaf') + the past/become verb شدی (classical past form) produce 'became deaf'. تیز گوش ('sharp/keen‑eared') is an epithet placed after the verb: 'the keen‑eared became deaf'—a poetic hyperbole meaning even the most attentive were stunned by his sound.
segments.s633-g7: به بهرام گفت = 'he said to Bahram' (به + proper name = to Bahram; گفت is the past verb 'said'—in the supplied sense list it appears as a noun, but here it functions as the past tense verb). ای پسندیده مرد is a vocative address: ای 'O', پسندیده 'chosen/pleasing' (epithet), مرد 'man/hero'—a direct address to Bahram.
segments.s633-g8: برآید is an impersonal/potential verb meaning 'it becomes possible' / 'may arise' (here: 'it can be done'). به دست تو = 'by your hand' where دست can mean 'hand' or 'power/control'—an agentive phrase indicating Bahram's agency. این کارکرد = 'this undertaking/deed' (این = 'this', کارکرد = 'performance/result'), the thing that can be accomplished.

sentences.s633-s5: When one came near that bird, the dragon's arrow pierced all his hide.
sentences.s633-s6: If because of him this land and realm become empty, let this victorious man be to your farr (glory).
sentences.s633-s7: May a helping hand be with you, near me — how near is this renowned assembly!
sentences.s633-s8: that your name would remain alive in the land of the Hindus for ever.
sentences.s633-s13: He kept saying for a while about his calmness, and about his height, breadth, and figure.
sentences.s633-s14: When he appeared and turned back, Bahram went, striding, into that thicket of down that had been scorched.
sentences.s633-s15: Then, behind him, several Iranians, in battle, bound that krag between them.
sentences.s633-s16: When from afar they saw his trunk, because of its commotion his homeland (or ground) was lowered.
sentences.s633-s33: Each chief advanced with offerings and said to Bahram, "O renowned one."
sentences.s633-s34: There is no one who merits you by deed; by your deeds there is no way to meet (you).
sentences.s633-s35: From him came joy/shadness and shangl, and a heart given to sorrow; sometimes fresh-faced and at other times sullen.
sentences.s633-s21: How would my death have been in another place, for thought was beyond measure.
sentences.s633-s22: The young man strung the bow, and you had said that the despised would seize the soul.
sentences.s633-s23: He came running until he was near the kerag, and, full of rage, he set his heart toward death.
sentences.s633-s24: He seized the Kayanian bow in his grip and drew from his quiver a dagger-like arrow.
sentences.s633-s17: To him everyone said, “O King, do not — this talk goes beyond (true) manliness.”
sentences.s633-s18: No one has fought a battle with mountain and rock; and although Khosrow is brave in (his) grip/hand,
sentences.s633-s19: Tell Shangal thus: this path is not (available); in this war there is no royal command (from the king).
sentences.s633-s20: He answered thus: ‘If the pure God had given me even the soil of India…’
sentences.s633-s25: Arrows kept falling like hail on those seated together until the kerk (bird) became sorrowful.
sentences.s633-s26: When he perceived that the appointed time had come, he rose and lifted a dagger in place of the bow.
sentences.s633-s27: He cut off the kerk's head cleanly, and said, 'In the name of God, the peerless and unmatched,'
sentences.s633-s28: for he has given me many a farr and might, and by his command they shine from the heaven of Hvar.
sentences.s633-s29: He ordered that they carry off the ox and the girdon, and that they bring the head of the ker[g] out of that grove.
sentences.s633-s30: They carried it; when Shengel saw from afar, he decked the banquet iwan with brocade.
sentences.s633-s31: When the rich/valiant king sat upon the throne, they seated Bahram before the royal presence.
sentences.s633-s32: Each person kept crying out “Bravo!” — thus did the Indian nobles and the horsemen.
sentences.s633-s9: Bahram the pure‑minded said to him that I must have a guide with me.
sentences.s633-s10: When I behold his body by God's power, you will see his shirt drenched in blood.
sentences.s633-s11: He gave him Shengel, a certain guide, whom he regarded as his seat and place.
sentences.s633-s12: He went with the kind‑hearted guide to that thicket where the (wolf/)creature was shedding blood.
sentences.s633-s1: There was a certain vulture in the king's city; from its back a streamer was tied that trailed in the wind.
sentences.s633-s2: From that thicket the male lion fled; and from the sky also came a feather‑winged vulture.
sentences.s633-s3: Every one in Hind, one by one, was filled with tumult because of him; even the keen‑eared were made deaf by his cry.
sentences.s633-s4: He said to Bahram, 'O chosen man, this deed can be accomplished by your hand.'
